name: Band Pallof Press
resistance_profile: Anti-rotation: the band pulls you toward a twist and you brace to resist it. Band tension and the anti-rotation demand are greatest with the arms pressed fully out.
cues: Keep hips and shoulders square to the front
Press straight out and resist the twist
execution_steps: Anchor a band at chest height and stand side-on, holding the loop with both hands at your sternum.
Step away from the anchor until the band is taut, feet shoulder-width, abs braced.
Press your hands straight out from your chest without letting your torso rotate toward the anchor.
Hold briefly at full extension, then return to your chest under control.
Finish the reps, then turn around and repeat facing the other way.
good_when: You train at home or travel with only bands
You want to teach anti-rotation bracing
bad_when: You want to load the abs heavily for hypertrophy (bands cap the tension)
You have no sturdy anchor point
common_errors: Letting the torso rotate toward the anchor
Pressing with the arms while the core stays slack
progressions: Step further from the anchor or use a stronger band
regressions: Step closer to the anchor for less tension
Hold the loop closer to the chest (shorter lever)
